source_title: OmniRetarget: Interaction-Preserving Data Generation for Humanoid Whole-Body Loco-Manipulation and Scene Interaction

ai_summary.one_liner: OmniRetarget turns a single human whole-body demonstration into interaction-preserving humanoid reference trajectories by optimizing an interaction mesh with hard physical constraints.
ai_summary.problem: Humanoid whole-body loco-manipulation needs human-like references, but common retargeting pipelines lose object/terrain contact structure and produce penetration or foot-skating artifacts that make downstream RL brittle.
ai_summary.solution: Build an interaction mesh over body, object, and terrain points, minimize Laplacian deformation while enforcing collision, joint, velocity, and foot-contact constraints, then re-solve the optimization under object, terrain, and embodiment augmentations.
ai_summary.key_result: The method generates 8+ hours of retargeted trajectories and enables Unitree G1 policies to execute up to 30-second parkour/loco-manipulation skills with only five reward terms and simple domain randomization; Table II reports 82.20% robot-object and 94.73% robot-terrain downstream RL success.
limitations: The current frame-by-frame optimization is effective but future work may need joint trajectory optimization for noisier motion sources such as video.
Some extremely difficult motions still expose limits of the simple RL formulation, motivating curriculum learning or richer policy training.
The paper reports strong Unitree G1 results, but cross-lab reproducibility depends on access to comparable mocap, simulation, robot, and evaluation infrastructure.

research_gaps.question: 재현성 한계: 결과는 Unitree G1, MoCap/OMOMO/LAFAN1, 시뮬레이션/하드웨어 파이프라인이 모두 맞물려 나온다. 다른 연구실이 동일한 hardware evaluation과 retargeting quality metric을 맞추기는 쉽지 않다.
